name: BSP Circular 1213 — Fraud Management System Requirements
summary: Requires all BSP-supervised financial institutions to deploy real-time fraud management systems with behavioral monitoring, blacklist screening, and geolocation capabilities.
requirements: Real-time behavioral anomaly detection for all digital transactions
Blacklist screening integrated into transaction flow
Geolocation monitoring for suspicious access patterns
Mobile device and account information change monitoring
Phase out SMS/email OTP for high-risk transactions
Fraud Management System must detect unauthorized transactions before completion
Integration with BSP's shared fraud intelligence network
penalties: Non-compliant institutions face BSP sanctions, potential license restrictions, and shared liability for fraud losses under Circular 1214.

sasCapabilityMapping.specificFeature: Integrated watchlist and blacklist matching engine
Location-based risk scoring with device fingerprinting
Event-driven rules triggering on profile changes

competitivePositioning: SAS Fraud Management is purpose-built for this circular. Competitors like Databricks/Snowflake require custom engineering. FICO Falcon and NICE Actimize also comply but at higher TCO and without the analytics platform breadth.
talkingPoints: SAS meets ALL Circular 1213 FMS requirements out-of-the-box — no custom development
June 30, 2026 deadline applies to ALL BSFIs — including rural banks with no current FMS
Non-compliance triggers shared liability under Circular 1214
SAS can go live in 8-12 weeks — critical given the approaching deadline
SAS is named as a Leader in Forrester Wave EFM APAC 2025
